NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and justice; To You, O LORD, I will sing praises.
NKJV.2: I will behave wisely in a perfect way. Oh, when will You come to me? I will walk within my house with a perfect heart.
NKJV.3: I will set nothing wicked before my eyes;  I hate the work of those who fall away; It shall not cling to me.
NKJV.4: A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will not know wickedness.
NKJV.5: Whoever secretly slanders his neighbor, Him I will destroy;  The one who has a haughty look and a proud heart, Him I will not endure.
NKJV.6: My eyes shall be on the faithful of the land, That they may dwell with me; He who walks in a perfect way, He shall serve me.
NKJV.7: He who works deceit shall not dwell within my house; He who tells lies shall not continue in my presence.
NKJV.8: Early I will destroy all the wicked of the land, That I may cut off all the evildoers from the city of the LORD.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. I will sing<S>7891</S> of mercy<S>2617</S> and judgment<S>4941</S>: unto thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, will I sing<S>2167</S>.
KJV.2: I will behave myself wisely<S>7919</S> in a perfect<S>8549</S> way<S>1870</S>. O when wilt thou come<S>935</S> unto me? I will walk<S>1980</S> within<S>7130</S> my house<S>1004</S> with a perfect<S>8537</S> heart<S>3824</S>.
KJV.3: I will set<S>7896</S> no wicked<S>1100</S> thing<S>1697</S> before mine eyes<S>5869</S>: I hate<S>8130</S> the work<S>6213</S> of them that turn aside<S>7750</S>; it shall not cleave<S>1692</S> to me. <sup>wicked: Heb. thing of Belial</sup>
KJV.4: A froward<S>6141</S> heart<S>3824</S> shall depart<S>5493</S> from me: I will not know<S>3045</S> a wicked<S>7451</S> person.
KJV.5: Whoso privily<S>5643</S> slandereth<S>3960</S> <S>3960</S> his neighbour<S>7453</S>, him will I cut off<S>6789</S>: him that hath an high<S>1362</S> look<S>5869</S> and a proud<S>7342</S> heart<S>3824</S> will not I suffer<S>3201</S>.
KJV.6: Mine eyes<S>5869</S> shall be upon the faithful<S>539</S> of the land<S>776</S>, that they may dwell<S>3427</S> with me: he that walketh<S>1980</S> in a perfect<S>8549</S> way<S>1870</S>, he shall serve<S>8334</S> me. <sup>in a: or, perfect in the way</sup>
KJV.7: He that worketh<S>6213</S> deceit<S>7423</S> shall not dwell<S>3427</S> within<S>7130</S> my house<S>1004</S>: he that telleth<S>1696</S> lies<S>8267</S> shall not tarry<S>3559</S> in my sight<S>5869</S>. <sup>shall not tarry: Heb. shall not be established</sup>
KJV.8: I will early<S>1242</S> destroy<S>6789</S> all the wicked<S>7563</S> of the land<S>776</S>; that I may cut off<S>3772</S> all wicked<S>205</S> doers<S>6466</S> from the city<S>5892</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>.

ESV.1: I will sing of steadfast love and justice; to you, O LORD, I will make music.
ESV.2: I will ponder the way that is blameless. Oh when will you come to me? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house;
ESV.3: I will not set before my eyes anything that is worthless. I hate the work of those who fall away; it shall not cling to me.
ESV.4: A perverse heart shall be far from me; I will know nothing of evil.
ESV.5: Whoever slanders his neighbor secretly I will destroy. Whoever has a haughty look and an arrogant heart I will not endure.
ESV.6: I will look with favor on the faithful in the land, that they may dwell with me; he who walks in the way that is blameless shall minister to me.
ESV.7: No one who practices deceit shall dwell in my house; no one who utters lies shall continue before my eyes.
ESV.8: Morning by morning I will destroy all the wicked in the land, cutting off all the evildoers from the city of the LORD.

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. I will sing of lovingkindness and justice, To You, O LORD, I will sing praises.
NASB.2: I will give heed to the blameless way. When will You come to me? I will walk within my house in the integrity of my heart.
NASB.3: I will set no worthless thing before my eyes; I hate the work of those who fall away; It shall not fasten its grip on me.
NASB.4: A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will know no evil.
NASB.5: Whoever secretly slanders his neighbor, him I will destroy; No one who has a haughty look and an arrogant heart will I endure.
NASB.6: My eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; He who walks in a blameless way is the one who will minister to me.
NASB.7: He who practices deceit shall not dwell within my house; He who speaks falsehood shall not maintain his position before me.
NASB.8: Every morning I will destroy all the wicked of the land, So as to cut off from the city of the LORD all those who do iniquity.

NLT.1:
I will sing of your love and justice, LORD.
I will praise you with songs.
NLT.2:
I will be careful to live a blameless life —
when will you come to help me?
I will lead a life of integrity
in my own home.
NLT.3:
I will refuse to look at
anything vile and vulgar.
I hate all who deal crookedly;
I will have nothing to do with them.
NLT.4:
I will reject perverse ideas
and stay away from every evil.
NLT.5:
I will not tolerate people who slander their neighbors.
I will not endure conceit and pride.
NLT.6:
I will search for faithful people
to be my companions.
Only those who are above reproach
will be allowed to serve me.
NLT.7:
I will not allow deceivers to serve in my house,
and liars will not stay in my presence.
NLT.8:
My daily task will be to ferret out the wicked
and free the city of the LORD from their grip.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. I will sing of loving kindness and justice. To you, Yahweh, I will sing praises.
WEB.2: I will be careful to live a blameless life. When will you come to me? I will walk within my house with a blameless heart.
WEB.3: I will set no vile thing before my eyes. I hate the deeds of faithless men. They will not cling to me.
WEB.4: A perverse heart will be far from me. I will have nothing to do with evil.
WEB.5: I will silence whoever secretly slanders his neighbor. I won’t tolerate one who is haughty and conceited.
WEB.6: My eyes will be on the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me. He who walks in a perfect way, he will serve me.
WEB.7: He who practices deceit won’t dwell within my house. He who speaks falsehood won’t be established before my eyes.
WEB.8: Morning by morning, I will destroy all the wicked of the land; to cut off all the workers of iniquity from Yahweh’s city.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of David. [1] I will sing of faithful love and justice;
I will sing praise to you, LORD. ⓚ
CSB17.2:
I will pay attention to the way of integrity.
When will you come to me?
I will live with a heart of integrity in my house. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
I will not let anything worthless guide me. [1]
I hate the practice of transgression;
it will not cling to me. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
A devious heart will be far from me;
I will not be involved with [2] evil. ⓝ
CSB17.5:
I will destroy anyone
who secretly slanders his neighbor; ⓐ
I cannot tolerate anyone
with haughty eyes or an arrogant heart. ⓑ
CSB17.6:
My eyes favor the faithful of the land
so that they may sit down with me.
The one who follows the way of integrity
may serve me. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
No one who acts deceitfully
will live in my palace;
the one who tells lies
will not be retained here to guide me. [3] ⓓ
CSB17.8:
Every morning I will destroy
all the wicked of the land,
wiping out all evildoers from the LORD’s city. ⓔ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. I will sing of [steadfast] lovingkindness and justice;
To You, O LORD, I will sing praises.
AMP.2:
I will behave wisely and follow the way of integrity.
When will You come to me?
I will walk in my house in integrity and with a blameless heart.
AMP.3:
I will set no worthless or wicked thing before my eyes.
I hate the practice of those who fall away [from the right path];
It will not grasp hold of me.
AMP.4:
A perverse heart shall depart from me;
I will not tolerate evil.
AMP.5:
Whoever secretly slanders his neighbor, him I will silence;
The one who has a haughty look and a proud (arrogant) heart I will not tolerate.
AMP.6:
My eyes will be on the faithful (honorable) of the land, that they may dwell with me;
He who walks blamelessly is the one who will minister to and serve me.
AMP.7:
He who practices deceit will not dwell in my house;
He who tells lies and half-truths will not continue [to remain] in my presence.
AMP.8:
Morning after morning I will destroy all the wicked in the land,
That I may cut off from the city of the LORD all those who do evil.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
My theme song is God's love and justice,
and I'm singing it right to you, God.
MSG.2:
I'm finding my way down the road of right living,
but how long before you show up?
I'm doing the very best I can,
and I'm doing it at home, where it counts.
MSG.3:
I refuse to take a second look
at corrupting people and degrading things.
I reject made-in-Canaan gods,
stay clear of contamination.
MSG.4:
The crooked in heart keep their distance;
I refuse to shake hands with those who plan evil.
MSG.5:
I put a gag on the gossip
who bad-mouths his neighbor;
I can't stand
arrogance.
MSG.6:
But I have my eye on salt-of-the-earth people—
they're the ones I want working with me;
Men and women on the straight and narrow—
these are the ones I want at my side.
MSG.7:
But no one who traffics in lies
gets a job with me; I have no patience with liars.
I've rounded up all the wicked like cattle
and herded them right out of the country.
MSG.8:
I purged God's city
of all who make a business of evil.

NIV.1:
Psalm 101
Of David. A psalm.
I will sing of your love and justice;
to you, O Lord, I will sing praise.
NIV.2:
I will be careful to lead a blameless life—
when will you come to me?
I will walk in my house
with blameless heart.
NIV.3:
I will set before my eyes
no vile thing.
The deeds of faithless men I hate;
they will not cling to me.
NIV.4:
Men of perverse heart shall be far from me;
I will have nothing to do with evil.
NIV.5:
Whoever slanders his neighbor in secret,
him will I put to silence;
whoever has haughty eyes and a proud heart,
him will I not endure.
NIV.6:
My eyes will be on the faithful in the land,
that they may dwell with me;
he whose walk is blameless
will minister to me.
NIV.7:
No one who practices deceit
will dwell in my house;
no one who speaks falsely
will stand in my presence.
NIV.8:
Every morning I will put to silence
all the wicked in the land;
I will cut off every evildoer
from the city of the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
I will sing of loyalty and of justice;
to you, O LORD, I will sing.
NRSVCE.2:
I will study the way that is blameless.
When shall I attain it?
I will walk with integrity of heart
within my house;
NRSVCE.3:
I will not set before my eyes
anything that is base.
I hate the work of those who fall away;
it shall not cling to me.
NRSVCE.4:
Perverseness of heart shall be far from me;
I will know nothing of evil.
NRSVCE.5:
One who secretly slanders a neighbor
I will destroy.
A haughty look and an arrogant heart
I will not tolerate.
NRSVCE.6:
I will look with favor on the faithful in the land,
so that they may live with me;
whoever walks in the way that is blameless
shall minister to me.
NRSVCE.7:
No one who practices deceit
shall remain in my house;
no one who utters lies
shall continue in my presence.
NRSVCE.8:
Morning by morning I will destroy
all the wicked in the land,
cutting off all evildoers
from the city of the LORD.

NET.1: I will sing about loyalty and justice! To you, O LORD, I will sing praises!
NET.2: I will walk in the way of integrity. When will you come to me? I will conduct my business with integrity in the midst of my palace.
NET.3: I will not even consider doing what is dishonest. I hate doing evil; I will have no part of it.
NET.4: I will have nothing to do with a perverse person; I will not permit evil.
NET.5: I will destroy anyone who slanders his neighbor in secret. I will not tolerate anyone who has a cocky demeanor and an arrogant attitude.
NET.6: I will favor the honest people of the land, and allow them to live with me. Those who walk in the way of integrity will attend me.
NET.7: Deceitful people will not live in my palace. Liars will not be welcome in my presence.
NET.8: Each morning I will destroy all the wicked people in the land, and remove all evildoers from the city of the LORD.
